title: Firmiana major
taxonomy: Firmiana major is classified under the family Sterculiaceae, which is a part of the larger order Malvales within the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the genus Firmiana, which is a group of deciduous trees native to tropical Asia. Firmiana major is also known as the Chinese Banyan or the Strangler Fig, although it is not a true fig. The species is native to southern China and Southeast Asia, where it is commonly found in tropical forests. Firmiana major is a large tree with a wide canopy and is often cultivated for its ornamental value.
uses: Firmiana major is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throat, and cough. Its leaves and branches are used to make a decoction that is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Additionally, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and promote wound healing. Firmiana major is also used in some folk remedies to treat skin conditions and respiratory problems.
benefits: Firmiana major has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including lowering blood pressure and reducing inflammation. The plant is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. Firmiana major may also have anti-diabetic properties, helping to regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. In addition, the plant has been used to treat various digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Firmiana major for medicinal use.

constituents: Firmiana major is known to contain active constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins. These compounds are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
